title: laburnum anagyroides f. serotinum

parts: Laburnum anagyroides f. serotinum has many medicinal parts, such as the flowers, which are rich in cytisine and anagyridine, two alkaloids known for their potential in treating respiratory issues. The leaves of this plant are said to contain laburnine, a toxic alkaloid that has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The roots of Laburnum anagyroides f. serotinum are reported to contain isopycaine and laburnamine, compounds that have been studied for their potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. side_effects: Laburnum anagyroides f. serotinum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain due to the presence of toxic compounds. Ingestion of the plant's seeds, which contain the toxic alkaloid cytisine, can lead to symptoms like tremors, confusion, and even seizures. If laburnum anagyroides f. serotinum is applied topically or used in traditional medicine, it may cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased heart rate.
